# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:2.7-slim

# Set environment variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install necessary packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    build-essential \
    libssl-dev \
    libffi-dev \
    libxml2-dev \
    libxslt1-dev \
    zlib1g-dev

# Copy application code into the container
COPY . /app
WORKDIR /app

# Install dependencies
RUN pip install --upgrade pip && pip install -r requirements.txt

# Set the command to run the application
CMD ["python", "app.py"]
